did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper did not use AI, machine learning, or deep learning to obtain its results. The study evaluated traditional image processing techniques, specifically average and Gaussian filters, for denoising skin cancer images. The methodology involved pre-processing (grayscaling, resizing, CLAHE) and adding artificial noise (Salt and Pepper, Gaussian) to images from the 'SkinLesion Analysis Toward Melanoma Detection' dataset, followed by metric analysis (PSNR, SSIM, SNR, MSE) to compare filter performance. While the introduction mentions deep learning (CNNs) as context for the field, the actual experimental work and results are based on conventional signal/image processing algorithms, not AI models.

non_compliance_reason: The paper does not use AI to obtain results in dermatology.
